summary:
Natalizumab has been proven effective in reducing relapses in relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis but provides no clinically demonstrated benefit in halting disability progression in secondary progressive multiple sclerosis due to its focus on immunomodulation rather than addressing underlying neurodegeneration. Therefore, it does not improve the disease course of SPMS.  

Final Answer: B
